name: ElevenLabs API
what_it_does: ElevenLabs' API for generating ultra-realistic text-to-speech audio, voice cloning, and real-time audio streaming using AI-powered voice synthesis.
use_cases: Generating natural-sounding voice output for conversational AI agents
Creating audio content from text for podcasts, videos, and narration
Building voice cloning for personalized AI assistants
Real-time voice synthesis for low-latency voice applications
Converting documents and articles to audiobook format
not_for: Very high volume TTS where cost is a concern
best_when: An agent needs to produce high-quality, natural-sounding voice output as part of a voice-enabled AI system or content generation workflow.
avoid_when: You need speech recognition rather than synthesis, or require on-device/offline voice.

auth.notes: Simple API key in xi-api-key header. Keys from ElevenLabs dashboard. Full account access per key. No scopes. Very easy for agents.

pricing.paid_tiers: Starter $5/month (30K chars), Creator $22/month (100K chars), Pro $99/month (500K chars)
pricing.notes: Free tier is limited but enough for prototyping. Character-based pricing is predictable. Voice cloning requires paid plan. Commercial use requires appropriate license tier.

performance.rate_limits: 2 concurrent requests on free tier, higher on paid

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON error responses with detail field. HTTP status codes are semantically correct. Error messages clearly indicate quota exhaustion and invalid voice IDs. Generally agent-friendly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Each TTS request generates a new audio file. No idempotency — identical requests produce similar but not identical audio.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier character limit exhausts quickly for agentic use cases
Voice IDs differ per account — cloned voices are not portable
Streaming audio uses chunked transfer encoding — agents need streaming HTTP client
Long text requires chunking — single requests have character limits per call
Voice stability and similarity settings significantly affect quality — agents need tuning
Commercial licensing for voice cloning requires verifying voice ownership
